body:
Split out of #195 (Tier-1 disk-fault harness) — the harness work stays in #195; this issue carries the **production reconstruction behaviour change** the harness flushed out, which was out of #195's scope.

## Problem

`reconstruction::assess` reads each placed fragment with `store.get_fragment(frag).await?` (`crates/custodian/src/reconstruction.rs:~247`). The `?` **propagates** any error, so a block-layer read fault (a `dm-error` / dead-sector `EIO`) on one placed fragment aborts the whole reconciliation — one faulted D server can stall repair for *every* chunk on the shared queue. A disk that goes bad *after* its data lands could therefore never be repaired.

The read path already tolerates this: `wyrd_core::read` admits only `if let Ok(Some(_))` (`crates/.../read.rs:~189`), reading an unreadable fragment around and rebuilding from the `k` survivors. Reconstruction should mirror that: a non-`NotFound` `get_fragment` error on a placed fragment is a **loss the rebuild reads around**, not a fatal error. `FsChunkStore::get_fragment` maps only `NotFound → Ok(None)` (`chunkstore-fs/src/lib.rs:~240`); a real faulted device returns `EIO → Err`.

## Correctness nuance to resolve (the reason this is its own issue, not a one-liner)

The candidate fix tried in #195 iteration 2 was `store.get_fragment(frag).await.ok().flatten()`. That is **too broad**: `.ok().flatten()` swallows *every* error as permanent fragment loss, not just block-layer read faults. A transient error from a **healthy** server would be misclassified as loss and trigger a permanent re-placement (the fragment is moved off the victim and its reference dropped) — risking spurious-rebuild churn and masking genuine store bugs. Unlike the read path's transient single-read read-around, reconstruction *permanently* moves the fragment.

The fix here should narrow the read-around to the right error class (e.g. distinguish a block-layer/IO read fault from a transient/healthy-server error) so a healthy-server transient does not cause a spurious permanent rebuild.

## Acceptance

- Reconstruction reads around an unreadable placed fragment (block-layer read fault) and rebuilds from survivors, rather than aborting the queue.
- A transient error from an otherwise-healthy server does NOT trigger a permanent re-placement.
- Promoted as a seeded regression per ADR-0009; exercised by the #195 Tier-1 harness's in-process EIO stand-in and the privileged `dm-error` scenario.

## References
- #195 iteration-1 carry-forward and iteration-2 advisory review (C5 / V, over-broad-swallow concern)
- `crates/custodian/src/reconstruction.rs` `assess`
- `crates/.../read.rs` `if let Ok(Some(_))` gate
- ADR-0009 (real-world discovery → seeded regression)
title: reconstruction::assess aborts repair on a get_fragment read fault instead of reading around it
